summary: Listener (registered Democrat, Rockville, Council D7, MD-19, MD-8) is now in choose-mode for the June 23 primary, not deciding-what-to-care-about-mode. Their April 28 note crystallizes it: 'now focus on who i can do on primary vote in June.' The dominant standing asks remain unchanged for a sixth straight week — full historical candidate dossiers, side-by-side policy comparisons across their actual ballot races, fiscal restraint as the operating value (property tax too high, non-essential spending should be cut), the Wootton High School relocation question, the new MCPS school rating system, and the sanctuary-policy litmus test. Today's coverage was a full four-episode series on Sharif Hidayat (Council D7 challenger to Luedtke). The Hidayat record is structurally thinner than Luedtke's because he has never held office; the show flagged that asymmetry on-air rather than papering over it.
themes: fiscal discipline vs. spending growth | Property tax too high (4/27) + non-essential spending cuts good (4/28) is now the listener's operating value across all races; today's Hidayat coverage flagged he has not staked an explicit property-tax-rate position, so the question is unsettled in District 7.

candidate full-history dossiers | Listener has asked for this every week from 4/25 onward; today's Hidayat dossier deliberately upgraded format (Egyptian-immigrant origin, MCPD timeline 1999–2022, ADL Shield 2012, FAA DPE designation, 3 campaigns in 5 years) per the 1800–3500 word target; on-air the show did not pretend his footprint was as deep as Luedtke's.

concrete side-by-side policy comparison across the listener's actual races | Today's ep4 introduced a Hidayat-vs-Luedtke split table covering Thrive 2050, housing mix, agricultural reserve, upcounty representation, closed primary, SROs, Trust Act / sanctuary posture, and most-recent property-tax decision; this is the format the listener has been asking for since 4/24.

MCPS structural decisions affecting them as a Rockville parent | Wootton relocation (4/29) and the new rating system (4/30) are still Board of Education questions; Hidayat ran for BoE in 2024 but has not commented publicly on either issue — flagged on-air as a forum question.

sanctuary policy as a litmus test | Honest answer for MoCo Democrats remains: no Democratic primary candidate currently before the listener has explicitly opposed the County's sanctuary posture on the record. Hidayat is on the record as pro-police strength but has not staked a sanctuary-policy position; Luedtke voted yes on Trust Act and helped sponsor the detention-center ban.
open_questions: Is Andrew Friedson the candidate who stands for fiscal restriction and spending discipline?
Why does MCPS want to close and move Wootton High School, and what happens next?
What is Kramer's full historical voting record, including old news beyond recent sessions?
What are the documented policy differences between each candidate on my primary ballot?
Which Democratic primary candidate actually opposes sanctuary policy on illegal immigration?
What is the new MCPS rating system actually doing — what changes for my kid?
What is Sharif Hidayat's stated position on the property-tax rate? (Open after today's series — he has not made one publicly.)
What is Sharif Hidayat's stated position on the Trust Act, the County detention-center ban, and the broader sanctuary posture? (Open after today's series.)
What military branch and dates of service correspond to Hidayat's self-identified 'Veteran' status? (The 1991–1999 window in his bio is undocumented in the public record reviewed.)
underserved_topics: Council At-Large field — listener votes for At-Large but no At-Large candidate has been profiled yet; remains tier-1 priority
MoCo Board of Education race — the Wootton and grading-system questions actually live here, but no BoE candidate has been profiled
MoCo State's Attorney, Sheriff, Clerk, Register, Orphans' Court — listener has voting rights and zero coverage
U.S. Representative MD-8 — Raskin race has five filed Democrats but no episode yet
Property-tax-rate trajectory and the FY26 spending affordability guidelines vote — listener's stated priority but not directly explained
How a registered Democrat in MoCo casts a vote against sanctuary policy in 2026 — answer remains the November ballot, not the June primary; the show has not yet said this plainly
avoid_list: The framing 'a listener asked us last week/earlier this week which Democratic primary candidates actually oppose sanctuary policy on illegal immigration' — used 4/28 ep1, 4/29 ep3
The Evan Glass DHS-screening bill as the sanctuary-policy hook — used 4/28 ep1, 4/29 ep3
The ICE agent mask-ban ordinance as the sanctuary-policy benchmark — used 4/29 ep2; today's series mentions it factually only
'Two stories, properly worked through' / 'two stories, not spreading thin across six' as an episode-framing device — 4/29 ep1
Governor Moore's 'Leave No One Behind' slogan contrasted with disability services cuts — 4/27 ep1 and ep1.rewrite
The ACA marketplace quiet-downgrade story — 4/28 ep1, 4/29 ep3
Maryland's 90-day part-time legislature explainer — 4/26 ep3 and ep3.rewrite
The wave of 25 Maryland lawmakers retiring or running for other offices — 4/26 ep1, ep1.rewrite, ep4
The Beltsville Agricultural Research Center closure as a District 19 candidate-record hook — 4/27 ep4 and ep4.rewrite
Listener's own frustration about promised candidate deep-dives not delivering — already narrated back across at least five episodes; do not repeat as a framing device
The Friedson cash-on-hand $2.1M cycle-to-date number with the Glass/Jawando comparison — 5/3 ep3; do not re-quote next week without a fresh report number
The labor-endorsement landscape paragraph (SEIU 500 + UFCW 27 to Jawando, MCEA to Glass, IAFF 1664 to Friedson) — 5/3 ep3; do not re-paste verbatim
The Sage at Shady Grove + More Housing N.O.W. illustration of Friedson's record — 5/3 ep3; do not repeat as framing
The Penn Glee Club / 'penndawn96' biographical hook — used heavily in 5/4 ep1 (Luedtke origins); do not redeploy in non-Luedtke episodes
The Battaglia first-clerkship anecdote — used as a Luedtke biographical anchor 5/4 ep1; do not redeploy outside Luedtke series
The 'red-headed stepchildren up here' Hidayat quote — used three times across today's 5/5 series (ep1, ep3, ep4); do not redeploy more than once next week
The 'first Muslim officer of MCPD' framing of Hidayat's 1999 swearing-in — used in 5/5 ep1 and ep2; do not lead a future episode with this line
The FAA Designated Pilot Examiner / Gaithersburg EA07 detail about Hidayat — used in 5/5 ep2; do not repeat verbatim
The 2012 ADL Shield Award detail about Hidayat — used in 5/5 ep2; do not repeat verbatim
The Hidayat-vs-Luedtke split-table format — used in 5/5 ep3 and ep4; do not redeploy as a head-to-head template within the next 7 days
